title: Reentrancy Vulnerability in setLiquidityReserve and setStabilizer
reason: Rejected. ERC20 balance moves in `_transfer()` do not invoke recipient callbacks, and the function makes no attacker-controlled external state-changing calls before updating storage.

title: Division by Zero in getFactor when _totalSupply becomes 0
reason: Rejected. This is a speculative terminal-state edge case without a concrete practical path in the reviewed source, and the contract is already bricked much earlier by missing initialization.

title: Arbitrary External Calls via Owner-Controlled Addresses
reason: Rejected. Setting reserve or stabilizer to a contract address does not by itself create arbitrary callback execution; token transfers only update internal accounting.

title: Taxless Setter Privilege Escalation Allows Fee Bypass
reason: Rejected. This is an intended owner-controlled privilege model, not a separate vulnerability absent compromise of a privileged role.

title: No Validation on Lock Box Unlock Time Bounds
reason: Rejected. The reviewed source contains no tranche-creation path, so there is no supported way to create a zero-unlock-time tranche from this code alone.

title: Owner Can Remap Lock Box Beneficiary After Vesting Period
reason: Rejected. `reassignTranche()` explicitly requires `unlockTime > now`, so reassignment after vesting is not allowed.

title: Missing Bounds Check on Tranche Index in getLockBoxes
reason: Rejected. Solidity array access reverts on out-of-bounds indices; it does not return default values.

title: Silent Pool State Updates Enable Price Manipulation
reason: Rejected as a standalone issue. The lack of an event is not the root bug; the real reportable problem is the manipulable balance-based oracle captured in F-003.

title: Out of Bounds Access in getSupportedPools
reason: Rejected. Solidity array indexing reverts on out-of-bounds access; it does not return `address(0)`.

title: Unlimited Minting During Presale Phase
reason: Rejected. The token does not itself enforce a presale cap, but presale authorization/cap logic may live in the external presale contract, and in this source presale minting is currently unreachable due to missing initialization.

title: Block Timestamp Dependency for Epoch Updates
reason: Rejected. This is ordinary timestamp-based epoch logic and does not rise to a realistic reportable issue on its own.
